system: CATCH-RNG — the throw + capture engine for rapp-go. A shrinking-ring throw, a Pokémon-style shake/break-free/flee sequence, and a catch probability that is a pure function of (the creature's deterministic rarity derived honestly from its genome content-hash) × (orb tier) × (aid/berry) × (throw quality). On a clean catch it mints the REAL .egg into the shared rapp-basket so it flows straight into cabinet/companion. Ships as one vendored, no-CDN, no-build module `rapp-go/catch.js` plus a runnable `rapp-go/catch.html` demo harness.
design:
THE FEEL. You've walked to a place; the ENCOUNTER surface has handed you a live .egg born from that place's sky. A capture RING pulses over the creature, shrinking large→small on a ~1.15s loop. You flick the orb: WHERE it lands = aim, the ring's size AT release = timing. Land inside the creature and you get a throw grade (normal / Nice / Great / Excellent). The orb sucks it in, the creature WOBBLES — one… two… three clicks — and either locks with a burst of sparkles ("kept"), or bursts back out ("it broke free!"), and on a break it may turn and vanish ("it fled — and took your orb"). Quiet, no arcade juice: soft haptic tick per wobble, a low chime on catch, a single grey puff on flee.

RARITY (deterministic, content-hash-honest). Rarity is a PURE FUNCTION of `cart.genome` — anyone can recompute it, no hidden server state, and the SAME egg id always has the SAME rarity. Two inputs, both from the genome:
  (a) a "specialness" score `s∈[0,1]` read from the genome's MEANING — because rare real weather makes rarer creatures ("gotta catch the weather"). Reading the layers that momentToGenome wrote:
      s=0; f=formLayer; su=surfaceLayer;
      if f.shape==='star'            s+=0.35   // weathercode≥71: SNOW
      if su.pattern==='stripe'       s+=0.30   // code≥80: showers/THUNDERSTORM
      if su.pattern==='spot'         s+=0.10   // rain
      if f.shape==='ring'            s+=0.06   // wet sky
      if (f.limbs||0)>=4             s+=0.12   // wind>30: gale
      if (f.segments||0)>=10         s+=0.10   // very windy
      if (su.glow||0)>=0.6           s+=0.15   // dead-clear sky OR bright full moon
      if su.palette?.[0]==='#ffffff' s+=0.20   // full-ish moon signature
      if su.palette?.join()==='#404060,#202040,#100020' s+=0.20 // new-moon signature
      s=clamp(s,0,1)
  (b) a uniform hash roll `hr∈[0,1)` so identical skies still vary: take the FULL sha256 hex of canonical(genome) (reuse genomeId's canonical() + sha256hex; slice(16,24)→int/2^32). Distinct slice from the 12-char id so it's uncorrelated.
  rarity01 = clamp(0.60*s + 0.40*hr, 0, 1). Buckets:
      <0.45 COMMON  | <0.68 UNCOMMON | <0.85 RARE | <0.95 LEGENDARY | else MYTHIC.
  Natural distribution: ordinary skies (s≈0) ride the hash → mostly Common/Uncommon with the odd Rare; snow/thunder/gale/full-moon skies get reliably pushed toward Rare+. The prize is a genuinely rare MOMENT, which is the soul.

PROBABILITY MODEL (per throw that HITS):
  p = clamp( baseCatch[tier] × orbMult × aidCatchMult × throwMult , 0.03 , 0.95 )
  baseCatch: COMMON .60 · UNCOMMON .42 · RARE .27 · LEGENDARY .15 · MYTHIC .08
  orbMult (Orb of Weather tiers): dew .5(training) · standard 1.0 · bright(Great) 1.5 · storm(Ultra) 2.0 · dawn(Master, near-lock) 6.0
  aidCatchMult (berries/aids, one active): none 1.0 · calm-berry 1.5 · honey 2.0 · golden 2.5
  throwMult (from ring size at release): normal 1.0 · Nice 1.15 · Great 1.5 · Excellent 1.85
  The 0.03 floor guarantees even a Master-orbless Mythic is never truly hopeless; the 0.95 ceiling keeps a shred of tension on everything.

SHAKE / BREAK-FREE (the classic 4-check decomposition, so p stays exact). Convert per-throw p into a per-wobble hold prob b = p^(1/4). Roll 4 independent checks:
  wobbles=0; caught=true;
  for i in 0..3: if rand()<b: wobbles++; else {caught=false; break;}
  → caught ⇔ all 4 pass (prob = b^4 = p, exactly). On a break it stopped at `wobbles`∈{0,1,2,3} — that's the "it shook twice then broke out!" you actually feel. rand() uses crypto.getRandomValues (honest, unpredictable) — ONLY rarity is deterministic, the roll never is.

FLEE (only evaluated on a break-free): fleeChance = clamp( baseFlee[tier] × aidFleeMult × orbHoldMult , 0 , 0.6 )
  baseFlee: COMMON .03 · UNCOMMON .06 · RARE .11 · LEGENDARY .18 · MYTHIC .25
  aidFleeMult: none 1.0 · calm-berry 0.5 · golden 0.35 (berries calm it AND boost catch — the strategic sink)
  orbHoldMult: higher-tier orbs hold slightly better on the bounce (standard 1.0 · storm .8 · dawn .4).
  If flee → encounter ENDS, orb already consumed, creature lost forever (that exact moment won't return — this is the stakes that make catching feel earned). If not flee → creature stays, throw again.

ORB CONSUMPTION & MISSES. A HIT (orb lands within the creature's screen bounds) always consumes one orb, then runs shakes. A MISS (bad aim) bounces the orb off-screen, does NOT consume it, no shake — a small mercy so fumbles don't drain inventory; the creature may jitter/step (encounter's job) making the next aim harder. Running out of orbs mid-encounter = you must leave (soft "it slipped away" — not counted as a flee, no genome lost, but that moment is gone too).

THROW QUALITY from the ring. Ring radius animates R(t)=Rmax·(0.35+0.65·(0.5+0.5·cos(2πt/1.15s))) — a smooth pulse, never fully collapsing. At release compute ratio q = R_now / R_creature (1.0 = ring hugs the creature):
  q≤1.10 Excellent(1.85) · ≤1.35 Great(1.5) · ≤1.75 Nice(1.15) · else normal(1.0).
  Aim: flick vector end-point; hit ⇔ inside creature bounding circle (with a forgiving 1.25× radius). MVP can collapse aim to auto-true and grade on ring-timing alone (a single tap), then layer the flick.

FEEDBACK (grounded, not loud): throw grade floats up as a hairline word; per-wobble → 40ms haptic tick + the creature's own birth-palette glow pulsing (reuse surface.palette so the shake is literally the creature's colours); catch → three ascending soft chimes + a ring of sparkles tinted by birth-palette + card slides into the basket strip; break-free → white burst, creature reappears; flee → desaturated puff, "it fled" in the void tint, orb counter ticks down. No numbers shown mid-throw — the wobble count IS the probability made physical.
integration:
CLOSES THE LOOP into the existing ONE gene pool:
1. BASKET: mintCaught → keepToBasket() writes to the SAME `rapp-basket` IndexedDB store the cabinet/basket/companion already read (db 'rapp-basket' v1, store 'eggs', keyPath 'id', record {id, egg, title, born, addedAt}). The instant a catch lands it appears in rapp-basket and in the cabinet's kept strip on next load — no new store, no migration.
2. CABINET (breed): because the cabinet auto-ingests basket eggs into `loadedCarts`, a caught weather-creature is immediately breedable via the existing crossBreed()/#adopt= flow. Offer a "🧬 breed this" button on the catch card → `hologram/index.html` (or rapp-cabinet) with `#adopt=<b64enc(JSON.stringify(cart))>` (b64enc reused verbatim) so even before it's synced it's in the pool.
3. COMPANION (talk): catch success shows "💬 talk to it" → `companion/index.html#token=<b64url(JSON.stringify(cart))>` (companion already parses `#token=` and bare `#<b64url>` into CART, injects it as the Companion's whole self). Its birth moment = the real place you walked to, so you can literally talk to the weather you caught.
4. LANTERN / player: the catch card's thumbnail + "open" link reuse `player.html#<b64enc(cart)>` (the universal player) — same render path as everywhere else.
5. UPSTREAM: ENCOUNTER hands catch.js a cart minted via the shared `momentToGenome()` + open-meteo fetch + `geohashEncode()`; CATCH-RNG needs nothing more than that cart (+ the player's current geohash for the `caught` stamp). INVENTORY (sibling system) owns orb/berry counts; catch.js only reads the chosen orb/aid keys and reports `consumedOrb`/whether an aid was spent so inventory can decrement. Clean seams: ENCOUNTER→cart, INVENTORY→{orb,aid}, CATCH-RNG→{caught,fled,consumedOrb}→BASKET.
ontaste: Soul over spectacle, three ways. (1) The rarity is HONEST: it's derived from the genome's content-hash + the real weather it encodes, so a Rare truly is a rare moment of a real sky — snow, a thunderstorm, a full moon over the park you walked to. You can't farm Mythics; you have to be somewhere real when the weather is. That's "gotta catch the weather," not "grind spawns." (2) The feedback is the creature's OWN self: wobbles pulse in its birth-palette, the catch sparkle is tinted by its birth sky, the flee puff is the void-tint — the ceremony is made of the moment, not generic arcade confetti. No score, no combo meter, no numbers mid-throw; the wobble count is the only "stat" and it's felt, not read. (3) The stakes are quiet but real: a flee loses that moment forever because that exact sky won't recur — which is true to life and makes a keep feel earned, then it flows into a thing you can talk to and breed. Loud games catch monsters; this keeps weather.
risks: Determinism vs fairness confusion — if the CATCH ROLL were deterministic from genome, a creature would be always- or never-catchable. Mitigation: ONLY rarity/catchRate is a pure function of genome; every roll uses crypto.getRandomValues, re-attempts are independent.

Content-hash honesty — stamping catch metadata inside `genome` would change the sha256 id and break 'this egg IS that moment'. Mitigation: `caught{}` provenance lives OUTSIDE genome (same pattern as home/lineage today); id is recomputed/verified equal to cart.id before minting.

Rarity tuning drift — the s-score reverse-infers weather from genome traits; if momentToGenome's mapping changes, the inference silently rots. Mitigation: keep the trait→rarity table adjacent to momentToGenome with a comment link, and a tiny self-test in catch.html that asserts known skies land in expected tiers.

Feel-balance — bad tables make Mythics impossible or Commons trivial. Mitigation: all tuning isolated in ORBS/AIDS/TIERS + two constants; ship a Monte-Carlo cell in catch.html that prints expected orbs-to-catch per tier×orb so a designer tunes empirically.

Frustration on flee — losing a Rare feels bad. Mitigation: flee only fires on a break-free (never first hit-if-you-graded-well), calm/golden berries roughly halve it, misses never consume orbs, and copy frames it as the moment passing (grounded), not a punish.
Battery/perf — rAF ring + (later) 3D player + camera can drain. Mitigation: ring is a cheap 2D canvas; cap DPR; pause rAF between encounters; the catch math is O(1).

Haptics/audio unsupported (iOS Safari gating) — navigator.vibrate is a no-op on iOS, WebAudio needs a user-gesture. Mitigation: feature-guard both; the throw gesture itself is the unlock; visual feedback (palette pulse/sparkle) is the primary channel, sound/haptics are additive.

No backend to prevent 'reroll until caught' cheese — a user can retry an encounter. Mitigation: acceptable for a single-player keepsake game; optionally bind an encounter's orb consumption to the shared inventory so cheesing still costs real (replenish-gated) orbs, preserving the economy without a server.
mvp: Smallest genuinely-playable slice, ~2 files, no map/AR/inventory needed: `rapp-go/catch.js` + `rapp-go/catch.html`. catch.html: (1) a 'Summon a sky' button calls the existing geolocation→open-meteo→momentToGenome path (copied from hologram/index.html) to mint ONE real .egg from your live weather; (2) eggRarity() grades it and shows its tier; (3) a shrinking capture RING over a placeholder disc (real 3D player dropped in later) — single TAP to throw, ring-size→throwQuality (aim auto-true for MVP); (4) rollCatch() runs the 4-check wobble loop with 420ms pacing, haptic tick + birth-palette glow per wobble; (5) resolves to CAUGHT (sparkle + keepToBasket write) or BREAK-FREE (throw again) or FLED (encounter ends, orb--); a fixed pouch of e.g. 5 standard + 2 storm orbs and 1 calm berry as a hardcoded stand-in for INVENTORY; (6) on catch, two buttons: '💬 talk to it' → companion#token=… and '🧬 breed' → cabinet#adopt=…. That is the entire catch loop — throw, shake, break/flee, mint a REAL egg into rapp-basket, and hand it to companion/cabinet — provably closing into the existing ecosystem, with map/POI/AR/full-inventory as later layers that only need to hand catch.js a cart + an {orb,aid}.
